channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: FBI Continues Review of Surveillance Footage in Search for Nancy Guthrie as Daughter Savannah Plans TV Return
item.description: The search for Nancy Guthrie, who has been missing from Tucson since February 1, is nearing its one-month mark, with no suspects identified. The FBI is currently reviewing up to 10,000 hours of video ...

item.title: Woman missing for 24 years located in North Carolina and arrested over outstanding DUI warrant
item.description: A woman who disappeared in December 2001 has been located in North Carolina after 24 years and arrested in connection with an outstanding drink-driving charge dating back to the time of her disappeara...

item.title: Instagram introduces alerts for parents when teens search for suicide or self-harm content
item.description: Instagram will begin alerting parents if their underage children repeatedly search for content related to suicide or self-harm. This change will affect users in the UK, US, Australia, and Canada who h...

item.title: Trump Offers No Clear Signal on Iran Strategy as Nuclear Talks Resume
item.description: US President Donald Trump declined to clarify his intentions toward Iran during his State of the Union address, despite heightened global tensions and an unprecedented American military build-up in th...

item.title: New DOJ Revelations Raise Questions About Epstein's Death and the Surveillance Footage
item.description: The death of Jeffrey Epstein remains one of the most debated and mysterious events of recent years, especially due to the strange circumstances surrounding the night of August 9, 2019. Three primary t...

item.title: Review comparison: Sage The Soft Top Luxe Kettle (£119) versus Salter Alchemy Rapid Boil Kettle (£39.99)
item.description: This Telegraph Recommended article, published on 26 February 2026, pits the premium Sage The Soft Top Luxe Kettle, priced at £119, against the budget-friendly Salter Alchemy Rapid Boil Kettle at £39.9...

item.title: Princess of Wales receives handmade necklace from young fans during community visits in Wales
item.description: During a visit to Wales ahead of St David's Day, Catherine, Princess of Wales, and Prince William engaged with local communities in Powys, celebrating volunteer work and creative initiatives. At the O...

item.title: Convicted Child Murderer Ian Huntley Seriously Injured in Prison Attack
item.description: Ian Huntley, the convicted child murderer responsible for the deaths of Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in 2002, remains in a critical condition in hospital following a violent attack at HMP Frankland...

item.title: PAHO warns of rising Chikungunya cases across the Americas
item.description: The Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) has issued a warning about the resurgence of Chikungunya virus across the Americas. Health authorities have observed a notable increase in reported cases in...

item.title: UK Government Faces Uncertainty Over Environmental Impact of Data Centres
item.description: The UK Energy Secretary, Ed Miliband, has acknowledged that the effect of the rapid expansion of data centres on the nation's efforts to achieve net zero carbon emissions by 2050 is 'inherently uncert...

item.title: Spain Releases 1981 Coup Attempt Files to Address Longstanding Conspiracy Theories
item.description: Spain’s government has declassified 153 documents related to the failed coup attempt of 23 February 1981, a defining episode in the country’s transition from dictatorship to democracy. On that day, ar...

item.title: Analysing the Impact of Labour’s Reforms on SEND Provision and Student Loan Policies
item.description: The podcast explores the recent government reforms affecting children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) and changes to student loan repayments. John Harris and Kiran Stacey discus...

item.title: Culture Secretary condemns racial slur aired during Bafta Awards broadcast
item.description: The BBC faced criticism after a racial slur was broadcast during the Bafta Film Awards. Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy described the incident as 'completely unacceptable and harmful' and has called for ...

item.title: Baroness Amos Interim Report Highlights Systemic Failures, Racism, and Bullying in England's Maternity and Neonatal Services
item.description: An interim report from the independent National Maternity and Neonatal Investigation in England, led by Baroness Amos, has revealed deep-rooted and persistent failings in maternity and neonatal servic...

item.title: Researchers Warn Accelerating AI Progress Is Outpacing Current Measurement Tools
item.description: Artificial intelligence is advancing at a pace that researchers say is becoming increasingly difficult to track accurately. According to evaluations conducted by the non-profit research institute METR...

item.title: Court hears YouTuber allegedly pre-recorded livestream used as alibi in partner’s murder
item.description: Stephen McCullagh, a 36-year-old YouTuber, is standing trial accused of murdering his 32-year-old pregnant partner, Natalie McNally, who was 15 weeks pregnant at the time. The prosecution alleges that...

item.title: Channel migrant crossings resume as 74 arrive in the UK after two-week pause
item.description: Migrants have reached the United Kingdom after crossing the English Channel for the first time in two weeks, according to reports and official data. On Wednesday, people wearing life jackets and coats...

item.title: Challenges and Solutions for E-Bike and E-Scooter Parking in UK Cities
item.description: The introduction of e-bike and e-scooter schemes in the UK has sparked both enthusiasm and frustration among city residents. While these schemes, which began with docked bicycles like London's Barclay...

item.title: UK experiences highest temperatures so far in 2026, breaking February records
item.description: The United Kingdom experienced its highest temperature of 2026 so far on Wednesday, February 25, with readings at Kew Gardens in London reaching 18.6°C. Other regions including Kent, Suffolk, Essex, N...

item.title: Impact of Second Home Restrictions on Rural Communities in Northumberland
item.description: Beadnell, a village on the Northumberland coast, was among the first in the UK to implement a ban on new homes being used as second homes or holiday lets, aiming to revitalize local communities. Nearl...
